tldr: Move from Veriff's identity verification SaaS to Pliance's developer-first AML / sanctions / PEP API stack focused on EU and Nordic compliance.
why_switch: Strong AML focus — sanctions, PEP and ongoing monitoring as first-class APIs
Swedish hosting and GDPR posture comfortable for EU fintechs
Combines CDD, screening and transaction monitoring in one stack
Better fit if you actually need AML rather than only document verification
what_to_export: Identity attributes (name, DOB, address, document references)
export_method: Request a data export through Veriff support and, in parallel, ensure all webhook payloads have already been persisted to your own database. Use your internal store as the source of truth during cutover. Consult Veriff's official documentation for current export procedures and retention obligations.
import_method: Plan Pliance as the AML / screening layer; if you also need document verification, keep that in a separate provider or evaluate whether Pliance's flows cover your needs. Wire customer onboarding to call Pliance's screening and monitoring APIs, persist case IDs against your user records, and subscribe to ongoing-monitoring alerts. Consult Pliance's official docs for current endpoints and SDK versions.

concept_mapping.note: Different primitive — Pliance is screening-centric, not document-centric
Confirm whether Pliance covers your document verification needs or pair with a separate KYC vendor
Different events: hits, status changes, ongoing alerts
Configure lists, thresholds and adverse-media settings
Map your user IDs onto Pliance customer records for ongoing monitoring
Pliance pricing is sales-led; budget time for procurement

gotchas: Pliance and Veriff overlap only partially — clarify which problem you are actually solving (KYC vs AML)
If you still need document verification, plan a hybrid stack
Custom contract means pricing transparency drops — negotiate exit terms up front
Ongoing monitoring creates new alert volume your ops team must handle
EU focus means non-EU coverage may be thinner — validate target geographies
Retain Veriff records as required by regulation even after migration

email.subject: Notice of non-renewal — Veriff
email.body:
Hello Veriff team,

We are migrating our compliance stack to a different provider and will not be renewing our current Veriff subscription.

Please confirm the effective end date, the process to export our verification records and audit logs, and the data deletion timeline after termination.

Thanks for the partnership.

Best regards,
